perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall pay half (1/2) of the purchase price prior to shipment and the remaining balance 30 days after shipment of the Products. Payments received after the 30 day calendar period will be subject to a late fee of 1.5% of the invoice amount.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall pay half (1/2) of the purchase price prior to shipment and the remaining balance 60 days after shipment of the Products. Payments received after the 30 day calendar period will be subject to a late fee of 1.5% of the invoice amount.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the payment terms. Section 4(b) initially states the remaining balance is due 30 days after shipment, but the modified version extends this to 60 days. However, the late fee clause still references a 30-day period, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about when late fees apply. This is because the 'Payments received after the 30 day calendar period will be subject to a late fee of 1.5% of the invoice amount' contradicts with the payment due date of '60 days after shipment of the Products'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Erchonia shall submit invoices to Distributor after or concurrent with shipment of Products to Distributor at the delivery address. Distributor shall pay half (1/2) of the purchase price prior to shipment and the remaining balance 60 days after shipment of the Products. Payments received after the 30 day calendar period will be subject to a late fee of 1.5% of the invoice amount. All payments shall be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the payment deadline to 60 days, but the late fee clause still refers to a 30-day period. This inconsistency creates a direct conflict within the document, as the 'Payments received after the 30 day calendar period will be subject to a late fee of 1.5% of the invoice amount' contradicts the payment due date of '60 days after shipment of the Products'. The contradicted text also contains both deadlines, making the contradiction more apparent.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall automatically renew for a period of three (3) years and upon the parties mutual agreement on new minimum performance goals for the renewal period.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall automatically renew for a period of one (1) year regardless of the parties mutual agreement on new minimum performance goals for the renewal period.
perturbation.explanation: The change creates an in-text contradiction with the initial term. It now states that 'This agreement shall automatically renew for a period of one (1) year regardless of the parties mutual agreement on new minimum performance goals for the renewal period.' This contradicts the original renewal term length of 'three (3) years' and remove the condition which was 'upon the parties mutual agreement on new minimum performance goals for the renewal period'. This impacts the duration of the agreement, making it unclear what the renewal period is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless terminated earlier as provided in this agreement, this Agreement shall have an initial term of three (3) years. This agreement shall automatically renew for a period of one (1) year regardless of the parties mutual agreement on new minimum performance goals for the renewal period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the renewal period from 'three (3) years' to 'one (1) year' while also removing the condition of 'mutual agreement on new minimum performance goals'. This creates a direct contradiction with the initial term stated in the contradicted text: 'Unless terminated earlier as provided in this agreement, this Agreement shall have an initial term of three (3) years.'. The modification directly contradicts the duration of the agreement's renewal, causing a significant inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Erchonia may revise its prices from time to time and deliver a written 90 day notice of the reasonable price revision to Distributor; provided that Erchonia agrees that the prices listed in Exhibit A shall not be increased during the first 90 days of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Erchonia may revise its prices from time to time and deliver a written 30 day notice of the reasonable price revision to Distributor; provided that Erchonia agrees that the prices listed in Exhibit A shall not be increased during the first 90 days of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the notice period for price revisions from 90 days to 30 days while retaining the clause that 'the prices listed in Exhibit A shall not be increased during the first 90 days of this agreement', a conflict is introduced. This is an in-text contradiction. Now, 'Erchonia may revise its prices from time to time and deliver a written 30 day notice of the reasonable price revision to Distributor' which contradicts with the condition which was 'the prices listed in Exhibit A shall not be increased during the first 90 days of this agreement.'. This could lead to disputes about when price changes can be implemented.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Erchonia agrees to provide Products to Distributor pursuant to orders placed by Distributor in the form of individual purchase orders issued by Distributor to Erchonia. Such purchase orders shall set forth the quantity of each product ordered, the required delivery date, and the point of delivery. The price for the Products shall be as designated in Schedule A. Erchonia may revise its prices from time to time and deliver a written 30 day notice of the reasonable price revision to Distributor; provided that Erchonia agrees that the prices listed in Exhibit A shall not be increased during the first 90 days of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 90-day notice for price revisions, aligning with the provision that prices in Exhibit A remain unchanged for the first 90 days. The changed text reduces the notice period to 30 days, creating a direct conflict. Erchonia can now provide a 30-day notice to revise prices, even though Exhibit A prices are supposed to be fixed for 90 days. The contradicted text, which includes the modified text, now contains conflicting statements, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections provide conflicting deadlines or obligations.
